contents.value:
```mdx
import { Button } from './components/Button'
import Chart from './Chart'
```
**import** brings JavaScript/React components into MDX. Must appear at the top of the file.

```mdx
import { useState } from 'react'
import styles from './page.module.css'
```
**from** specifies the module path: npm packages, relative paths, or aliased paths.

```mdx
import { Button as PrimaryButton } from './components'
import * as Icons from './icons'
```
**as** renames an import binding to avoid naming conflicts.

```mdx
# My Article

Some markdown text.

<Button variant="primary" onClick={() => alert('clicked')}>
  Click me
</Button>
```
**JSX** can be used inline with Markdown in MDX. Components must be imported or provided via MDXProvider.

```mdx
# Hello {name}

The result is {2 + 2}.

Today is {new Date().toLocaleDateString()}.
```
**Expressions** in `{}` embed JavaScript values and logic inline. Supports variables, arithmetic, and conditionals.

```mdx
---
title: Getting Started
description: Learn MDX basics
author: Jane Doe
date: 2024-01-15
---
```
**Frontmatter** is YAML metadata between `---` delimiters at the top of the file.

```jsx
import { MDXProvider } from '@mdx-js/react'

const components = {
  h1: props => <h1 className="title" {...props} />,
  code: CodeBlock,
  a: CustomLink
}

<MDXProvider components={components}>{children}</MDXProvider>
```
**MDXProvider** supplies custom components that replace default HTML elements in MDX.

```mdx
import { Tabs, Tab } from './Tabs'
import { Callout } from './Callout'

<Callout type="warning">
  This is important!
</Callout>
```
**Components** are React elements used directly in MDX for interactive, reusable content blocks.

```mdx
# H1 Title
## H2 Section
### H3 Subsection
## Dynamic {version}
```
**Headings** use standard Markdown `#` syntax. Can contain JSX expressions in MDX.

````mdx
```javascript title="example.js" {3-4} showLineNumbers
function hello() {
  console.log('Hello')
}
```
````
**Code blocks** use triple backticks with optional language, title, and line highlighting meta.

```mdx
Use the `useState` hook for state management.
```
**Inline code** uses single backticks. Can be customized via MDXProvider.

```mdx
[External link](https://example.com)
[Internal link](/docs/getting-started)
```
**Links** use standard Markdown syntax. Internal links are handled by framework routers.
```mdx ![Alt text](./image.png) import screenshot from './screenshot.png' ``` **Images** use Markdown `![]()` or JSX ``. Import images for bundler optimization.

```mdx
| Feature | Supported |
|---------|:---------:|
| Markdown | Yes |
| JSX | Yes |
```
**Tables** use pipe-delimited Markdown syntax. Colon placement controls alignment.

```mdx
- Item one
  - Nested item
1. First step
- [ ] Todo incomplete
- [x] Todo complete
```
**Lists** support unordered, ordered, and task list syntax. Can contain JSX as list items.

```mdx
> Standard blockquote text.
> > Nested quote
```
**Blockquotes** use `>` prefix. Consider custom Callout components for better styling.

```mdx
*italic* or _italic_
**bold** or __bold__
***bold italic***
~~strikethrough~~
```
**Text emphasis** uses standard Markdown formatting syntax.

```mdx
---
```
**Horizontal rule** creates a thematic break. Must have blank lines before and after.

```javascript
{
  remarkPlugins: [remarkGfm, remarkMath, remarkToc],
  rehypePlugins: [rehypeSlug, rehypeHighlight]
}
```
**Plugins** extend MDX processing. Remark transforms Markdown AST, rehype transforms HTML AST.

```mdx
Inline math: $E = mc^2$

$$
\sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i
$$
```
**Math** support requires `remark-math` and `rehype-katex` plugins. Uses LaTeX syntax.

```mdx
~~strikethrough~~
https://auto.link
- [x] task list
| table | support |
```
**GFM** (GitHub Flavored Markdown) adds autolinks, tables, task lists, strikethrough. Enable with `remark-gfm`.

```mdx
import { Tabs, Tab } from 'nextra/components'

<Tabs items={['npm', 'yarn', 'pnpm']}>
  <Tab>npm install package</Tab>
  <Tab>yarn add package</Tab>
  <Tab>pnpm add package</Tab>
</Tabs>
```
**Tabs** component shows switchable panels for alternatives.

```mdx
import { Steps } from 'nextra/components'

<Steps>
### Install dependencies
npm install mdx
### Create your first file
Create hello.mdx
</Steps>
```
**Steps** renders numbered step-by-step instructions.

```mdx
import { Callout } from 'nextra/components'

<Callout type="warning">
  Be careful with this operation.
</Callout>
```
**Callout** displays styled admonition blocks: `info`, `warning`, `error`, `default`.

```mdx
import { Cards, Card } from 'nextra/components'

<Cards>
  <Card title="Getting Started" href="/docs/start" />
  <Card title="API Reference" href="/docs/api" />
</Cards>
```
**Cards** renders a grid of navigation cards for landing pages.

```mdx
import { FileTree } from 'nextra/components'

<FileTree>
  <FileTree.Folder name="src" defaultOpen>
    <FileTree.File name="index.ts" />
  </FileTree.Folder>
</FileTree>
```
**FileTree** renders an interactive file/folder tree visualization.

```javascript
import remarkGfm from 'remark-gfm'

export default {
  remarkPlugins: [remarkGfm]
}
```
**remark-gfm** enables GitHub Flavored Markdown: tables, task lists, autolinks, strikethrough, footnotes.

```javascript
import { compile } from '@mdx-js/mdx'
const code = await compile('# Hello', {
  outputFormat: 'function-body'
})
```
**compile** transforms MDX source into JavaScript.

```javascript
import { evaluate } from '@mdx-js/mdx'
import * as runtime from 'react/jsx-runtime'
const { default: Content } = await evaluate('# Hello', runtime)
```
**evaluate** compiles and runs MDX at runtime. Useful for CMS or dynamic content.

```mdx
:::note
This is a note directive.
:::

:::tip[Custom title]
Helpful tip here.
:::
```
**Directives** (via `remark-directive`) add container blocks. Used by Docusaurus.

```mdx
import { TOC } from './components/TOC'
<TOC />

## Introduction
## Getting Started
```
**Table of Contents** can be auto-generated from headings or rendered with custom components.

```mdx
<iframe src="https://codesandbox.io/embed/example" width="100%" height="500" />

import { YouTube } from './components/YouTube'
<YouTube id="dQw4w9WgXcQ" />
```
**Embeds** use JSX to include iframes, videos, or interactive demos.

```mdx
import { Aside } from './components/Aside'

<Aside>
  **Related:** See the [config guide](/docs/config).
</Aside>
```
**Aside** renders supplementary content as a sidebar or margin note.

```mdx
export default (props) => <Layout>{props.children}</Layout>
```
Defines a default export wrapper component that controls the layout of the entire MDX document, receiving the rendered content as children for custom page structure.

```mdx
<Component prop={value}>children</Component>
```
Embeds a JSX or React component directly within markdown content, passing props and children. Components are resolved from imports or the MDXProvider component mapping context.

```mdx
remarkPlugins: Plugin[]
```
Configuration option specifying remark plugins that transform the markdown AST before compilation, enabling features like GitHub-flavored markdown, math expressions, and custom syntax extensions.

```mdx
rehypePlugins: Plugin[]
```
Configuration option specifying rehype plugins that transform the HTML or JSX AST after markdown processing, enabling features like syntax highlighting, heading anchors, and HTML sanitization.

```mdx
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```mdx
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```mdx
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```mdx
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```mdx
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```mdx
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```mdx
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```mdx
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```mdx
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
